title: cirrus: never run functional tests on rhel branches
body:
As discussed at the cabal October 8, 2024 we have no need for these tests on RHEL branches. The work to maintain them is higher than it is worth it. We also do not test RHEL but rather some outdated frozen fedora image build from the time we created the branch.

Therefore we gain little value from them especially as all the interal Red Hat QE is testing it anyways again on the proper RHEL builds.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Ported to v5.2-rhel in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24349 and it seems to work, only build tasks left there.

@edsantiago PTAL, unfortunatly even more duplication...

What is the purpose behind this? Is it meant to apply only to v5.2-rhel and all future RHEL branches cut from this point forward? If so, might it not be simpler to add an item to the branching checklist, to excise all such tasks from `.cirrus.yml`? That could even be scripted.

> What is the purpose behind this? Is it meant to apply only to v5.2-rhel and all future RHEL branches cut from this point forward? 

Yes as discussed we only want build/validate tasks being run on rhel branches so yes this should just work going forward.

> If so, might it not be simpler to add an item to the branching checklist, to excise all such tasks from `.cirrus.yml`? That could even be scripted.

Well yes and no, simpler for our yaml for sure but nobody follows the proper process (even with docs) as such the person creating the branch will not run this script. People even forget to update DEST_BRANCH all the time so I have little trust in that. I rather do it here once on main and not remind people every 6 months that they have to turn this off.

To be clear I am open to go with the delete route if others like that better.

@mheon WDYT? As you are the one creating most of these branches.

Also I will be looking in [starlark](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/programming-tasks/) soon anyway for the other parts of ci changes (i.e. moving some tests only to nightly and on demand) so I am hopeful that I can remove duplication and simplify the conditions with that.

The -rhel branches are usually just a force-push of a new branch based off a tag. Adding an extra step of "replace cirrus.yml with this super-minimal version and PR that" seems fine. Not a significant added burden.
